text.title: Cohort-Based Mini-Course Launch Core Rules
text.summary: 5 testable rules every Cohort-Based Mini-Course Launch application must enforce. These are HARD rules — user-supplied input cannot override them.

rule.statement: First cohort: 15-30 seats. Under 15 = thin discussion; over 30 = unmanageable feedback load for solo instructor.
rule.rationale: Empirical Maven/Wes Kao cohort data: 20-25 is the sweet spot for solo-instructor + meaningful discussion.

rule.statement: Course runs 4-6 weeks, ≤6h student commitment per week. Longer = drop-off; shorter = insufficient transformation.
rule.rationale: Cohort retention curves drop sharply past 6 weeks; <4 weeks fails to deliver real outcome.

rule.statement: Price: hourly_consulting_rate × total student hours × 0.10 to 0.30. Below 10% undervalues; above 30% prices out indie students.
rule.rationale: Anchor-driven pricing avoids both undercharging (the indie default) and overcharging (the bootcamp default).

rule.statement: Pick a stack: Maven (community + payments + cohort tools, 10% fee) OR Podia+Circle (separate, own community). Avoid mixing 3+ tools for first cohort.
rule.rationale: Platform fragmentation eats organizer time; first cohort needs less ops, not more.

rule.statement: Course doubles as lead-magnet: alumni → email list → next cohort + recurring product upsell. Capture alumni emails + opt-in for future.
rule.rationale: Single-shot courses earn one revenue cycle; lead-magnet tail compounds over years.
